goal: Increase the maximum practical waveform detail in the timeline by expanding high-zoom behavior and making sample-level waveform rendering kick in more aggressively for close zoom levels.
constraints: Modify only src/renderer/src/components/Timeline.tsx, src/renderer/src/components/WaveformRenderer.tsx, and src/main/waveform/waveformAnalysisService.ts unless a tiny adjacent type/import adjustment is strictly required.
Preserve the existing playhead-anchored zoom behavior, playback logic, and current dark Omega visual style.
Do not remove the existing waveform caching/windowing architecture; refine it.
Favor a clearly higher maximum zoom range, stronger zoom acceleration at high zoom, and earlier sample-mode usage where it improves visible detail.
Keep the implementation conservative enough to avoid obvious white-canvas regressions.
Run npm run typecheck before finishing.
success_criteria: The timeline can zoom in substantially further than before, beyond the current hard cap of 20.
Wheel and button zooming feel faster at high zoom levels so the user needs fewer steps to reach extreme detail.
Waveform rendering switches into fine-grained sample rendering earlier or more reliably at close zoom levels, producing more distinct small waveform detail.
The implementation stays scoped to the zoom/rendering pipeline and remains consistent with the existing code style.
